hero.tagline: Nebraska's small claims court offers a streamlined process for resolving disputes up to $7,500 without attorney representation.

at_a_glance.label: Most you can sue for
at_a_glance.detail: Same cap for individuals and businesses

annual_filing_cap.notes: No more than 2 cases per week or 10 per calendar year.

limits.split_claims_explanation: If a claim exceeds $7,500, you cannot split it into multiple small claims cases; you must file in a higher court.

claim_categories.title: Contracts and money owed
claim_categories.blurb: Nebraska allows you to file claims for breaches of contract and money owed up to $7,500. The statute of limitations is 5 years for written contracts and 4 years for oral contracts.

claims.name: Breach of a written contract
claims.example: A contractor signed a $4,000 contract to remodel your kitchen, took the deposit, and never finished.
claims.notes: 5-year deadline from breach. Bring the signed contract, payment proof, and any correspondence showing the breach.

claims.name: Breach of an oral contract
claims.example: You agreed verbally to lend someone $2,000, and they failed to repay it.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from breach. Provide evidence of the agreement, such as emails or witness testimony.

claims.name: Unpaid personal loan
claims.example: You loaned a friend $1,500 with the understanding they'd repay in 6 months, but they haven't.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from breach. Gather any written agreement, payment records, and communication regarding the loan.

claims.name: Default on a promissory note
claims.example: Someone signed a promissory note to repay $5,000 but missed payments.
claims.notes: 5-year deadline from breach. Present the original promissory note and records of missed payments.

claims.name: Unpaid invoice for services rendered
claims.example: You provided $3,000 worth of consulting services, but the client hasn't paid the invoice.
claims.notes: 5-year deadline from breach. Bring the invoice, service agreement, and any communication regarding payment.

claims.name: Unpaid balance on an open account
claims.example: A customer has an outstanding balance of $2,500 on their account with your business.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from breach. Provide account statements and records of transactions.

claims.name: Unpaid rent by tenant
claims.example: Your tenant owes $1,200 in unpaid rent for the past two months.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from breach. Bring the lease agreement, payment records, and any notices sent to the tenant.

claims.name: Recovery of funds from a bad check
claims.example: A customer paid with a $500 check that bounced due to insufficient funds.
claims.notes: Provide the original check, bank notice of insufficient funds, and any communication with the issuer.
claims.damage_boost: Check amount + service charge up to $40 + civil penalty up to $100 OR 2x check amount, whichever is greater.

claims.name: Co-signer seeking repayment from primary borrower
claims.example: You co-signed a $4,000 loan, and the primary borrower defaulted, leaving you responsible.
claims.notes: 5-year deadline from breach. Bring the loan agreement, proof of payments made, and any communication with the borrower.

claim_categories.title: Consumer disputes
claim_categories.blurb: Nebraska's small claims court handles various consumer disputes up to $7,500, including defective products and poor service.

claims.name: Purchase of a defective product
claims.example: You bought a $1,000 appliance that stopped working within a month, and the seller refuses to refund or replace it.
claims.notes: Bring the purchase receipt, warranty information, and any communication with the seller.

claims.name: Substandard services rendered
claims.example: You paid $2,500 for home repairs that were poorly done, and the contractor refuses to fix them.
claims.notes: Provide the service contract, photos of the work, and any correspondence with the contractor.

claims.name: Dispute over auto repair charges
claims.example: A mechanic charged you $1,200 for repairs that didn't fix the problem, and refuses a refund.
claims.notes: Bring the repair invoice, any warranties, and communication with the mechanic.

claims.name: Contractor failed to complete agreed work
claims.example: You paid a contractor $3,500 to build a deck, but they abandoned the project halfway.
claims.notes: Provide the contract, payment records, and evidence of incomplete work.

claims.name: Vendor failed to deliver paid goods/services
claims.example: You paid $500 for catering services, but the vendor never showed up.
claims.notes: Bring the contract, payment proof, and any communication with the vendor.

claims.name: Unauthorized charges after subscription cancellation
claims.example: You canceled a $50/month gym membership, but they continued to charge your account.
claims.notes: Provide cancellation confirmation, bank statements, and any correspondence with the gym.

claims.name: Purchase of a defective used car
claims.example: You bought a used car for $6,000 that broke down within a week, and the seller refuses to address the issue.
claims.notes: Bring the sales agreement, any warranties, and repair records.

claims.name: Misleading advertising leading to financial loss
claims.example: A store advertised a product for $200, but charged you $300 at checkout without notice.
claims.notes: Provide the advertisement, receipt, and any communication with the store.

claim_categories.title: Property damage
claim_categories.blurb: Nebraska's small claims court addresses property damage claims up to $7,500, including vehicle collisions and damage to rental properties.

claims.name: Damage from a vehicle collision
claims.example: Another driver hit your car, causing $4,000 in damages, and refuses to pay.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from incident. Bring the police report, repair estimates, and photos of the damage.

claims.name: Neighbor caused damage to your property
claims.example: Your neighbor's tree fell on your fence, causing $1,500 in damages.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from incident. Provide photos, repair estimates, and any communication with the neighbor.

claims.name: Tenant caused damage to rental property
claims.example: A tenant left your rental unit with $2,000 in damages beyond normal wear and tear.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from incident. Bring the lease agreement, photos of damages, and repair invoices.

claims.name: Damage caused by someone else's pet
claims.example: A neighbor's dog damaged your $800 garden fence.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from incident. Provide photos, repair estimates, and any communication with the pet owner.

claims.name: Damage to property while in someone else's custody
claims.example: You left your laptop for repair, and it was returned with a cracked screen.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from incident. Bring the service agreement, photos of damage, and any communication with the service provider.

claim_categories.title: Landlord-tenant disputes
claim_categories.blurb: Nebraska's small claims court handles monetary disputes between landlords and tenants up to $7,500, excluding eviction proceedings.

claims.name: Failure to return security deposit
claims.example: Your landlord withheld your $1,200 security deposit without justification.
claims.notes: Provide the lease agreement, move-out inspection report, and any communication with the landlord.

claims.name: Landlord failed to provide itemized deductions from deposit
claims.example: Your landlord deducted $500 from your deposit without providing an itemized list of damages.
claims.notes: Bring the lease agreement, correspondence with the landlord, and any evidence of the property's condition.

claims.name: Landlord failed to maintain habitable conditions
claims.example: Your apartment had no heating during winter, and the landlord refused to fix it.
claims.notes: Provide photos, repair requests, and any communication with the landlord.

claims.name: Landlord retained tenant's personal property unlawfully
claims.example: After moving out, your landlord kept your $800 bicycle without cause.
claims.notes: Bring the lease agreement, inventory of items, and any communication with the landlord.

claims.name: Landlord conducted an illegal eviction
claims.example: Your landlord changed the locks without notice, preventing you from accessing your belongings.
claims.notes: Provide the lease agreement, photos, and any communication with the landlord.

claims.name: Landlord failed to return prepaid rent after lease termination
claims.example: You paid first and last month's rent, but the landlord didn't return the last month's rent after early termination.
claims.notes: Bring the lease agreement, payment records, and any communication with the landlord.

claims.name: Tenant failed to pay rent owed
claims.example: Your tenant owes $2,000 in unpaid rent and has vacated the property.
claims.notes: Provide the lease agreement, payment records, and any notices sent to the tenant.

claims.name: Tenant seeks reimbursement for repairs made
claims.example: You paid $300 to fix a leaking pipe after the landlord failed to address it.
claims.notes: Bring repair receipts, photos, and any communication with the landlord.

claim_categories.title: Employment and wage disputes
claim_categories.blurb: Nebraska's small claims court addresses wage-related disputes up to $7,500, including unpaid wages and final paychecks.

claims.name: Employer failed to pay earned wages
claims.example: Your employer owes you $1,000 for hours worked but hasn't paid.
claims.notes: Provide pay stubs, timesheets, and any communication with the employer.

claims.name: Employer failed to provide final paycheck
claims.example: After leaving your job, your employer didn't give you your last paycheck of $800.
claims.notes: Bring employment records, termination notice, and any communication with the employer.

claims.name: Employer failed to pay earned commissions
claims.example: You earned $2,500 in sales commissions that your employer hasn't paid.
claims.notes: Provide sales records, commission agreements, and any communication with the employer.

claims.name: Employer failed to pay overtime wages
claims.example: You worked 10 hours of overtime but weren't compensated at the correct rate.
claims.notes: Bring timesheets, pay stubs, and any communication with the employer.

claim_categories.title: Personal claims
claim_categories.blurb: Nebraska's small claims court handles personal claims up to $7,500, including minor injuries and property disputes.

claims.name: Minor personal injury claims
claims.example: You slipped on a wet floor in a store, resulting in $2,000 in medical bills.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from incident. Provide medical records, bills, and any incident reports.

claims.name: Failure to return loaned property
claims.example: You lent a friend your $500 camera, and they refuse to return it.
claims.notes: 4-year deadline from incident. Bring evidence of ownership and any communication regarding the loan.

claims.name: Theft of personal property
claims.example: Someone stole your $1,200 bicycle, and you have evidence of their involvement.
claims.notes: Provide police reports, evidence of theft, and any communication with the defendant.

claim_categories.title: Vehicle-specific disputes
claim_categories.blurb: Nebraska's small claims court addresses vehicle-related disputes up to $7,500, including mechanic disputes and wrongful towing.

claims.name: Dispute over mechanic's repair work
claims.example: A mechanic charged you $1,500 for repairs that didn't fix the issue.
claims.notes: Bring the repair invoice, any warranties, and communication with the mechanic.

claims.name: Vehicle was wrongfully towed
claims.example: Your car was towed from a legal parking spot, and you had to pay $300 to retrieve it.
claims.notes: Provide photos of the parking spot, towing receipt, and any communication with the towing company.

exclusions.explanation: Small claims court cannot handle eviction proceedings; these must be filed in county court.
Disputes involving ownership or title to real property are beyond the jurisdiction of small claims court.
Claims involving defamation, such as libel or slander, are not suitable for small claims court.
Professional malpractice claims require complex evidence and are not handled in small claims court.
Issues like divorce, child custody, and support are outside the scope of small claims court.
Disputes involving wills, estates, and trusts are not handled in small claims court.
Small claims court does not have jurisdiction over cases against federal entities.
These claims are handled by the Nebraska Workers' Compensation Court, not small claims court.
Small claims court is not equipped to handle class action lawsuits.
Small claims court cannot issue orders requiring someone to do or stop doing something.

damages.punitive_explanation: Nebraska small claims court does not award punitive damages; however, statutory penalties may apply in certain cases.

statutory_multipliers.explanation: Check amount + service charge up to $40 + civil penalty up to $100 OR 2x check amount, whichever is greater.

damages.attorney_fees_explanation: Attorney's fees may be awarded in cases involving bad checks or civil theft under specific statutes.

where_to_file.court_name: Small Claims Division of the County Court

filing_options: Any county where ONE defendant resides
Where the cause of action arose

where_to_file.consequences_of_wrong_venue: Filing in the wrong venue may result in dismissal or transfer of the case, potentially causing delays and additional costs.
where_to_file.e_filing_notes: E-filing availability varies by county; check with the local court clerk for options.

frequent_filer.notes: No more than 2 cases per week or 10 per calendar year.

forms.name: Application to Proceed In Forma Pauperis

eligibility_bullets: Receiving need-based public assistance (SNAP, TANF, SSI)
Household income below 125% federal poverty line
waiver.coverage_notes: Waiver covers filing fees and service costs.
waiver.if_you_win_notes: Waived fees are not added to the judgment against the loser.

fees.fees_recoverable_from_loser_notes: If you win, filing fee and reasonable service costs are added to the judgment.

statute_of_limitations.discovery_rule_notes: The discovery rule applies to fraud claims, starting the clock when the fraud is discovered.
statute_of_limitations.tolling_notes: The statute of limitations may be tolled if the defendant is out of state, the plaintiff is a minor, or in cases of bankruptcy.

pre_filing.demand_letter_notes: While not required, sending a demand letter can often resolve disputes without court intervention.
pre_filing.bad_check_notice_rule: A 30-day written notice is required before pursuing a bad check claim, allowing the issuer to make the check good.
pre_filing.wage_claim_demand_rule: A written demand for unpaid wages is recommended before filing a claim.
pre_filing.landlord_tenant_pre_filing_notes: Tenants should provide a forwarding address in writing to ensure the return of the security deposit.
pre_filing.consumer_protection_notes: No specific pre-filing requirements for consumer protection claims in small claims court.

forms.name: Plaintiff’s Claim and Notice to Defendant
forms.description: Initiates a small claims case by outlining the plaintiff's claim and notifying the defendant.

forms.name: Defendant’s Counterclaim
forms.description: Allows the defendant to file a counterclaim against the plaintiff.

forms.name: Application to Proceed In Forma Pauperis
forms.description: Requests a waiver of court fees due to financial hardship.

forms.name: Satisfaction of Judgment
forms.description: Filed by the plaintiff to indicate that the judgment has been satisfied.

forms.name: Notice of Appeal
forms.description: Filed to initiate an appeal of the small claims court decision.

methods.name: Sheriff personal service
methods.description: Sheriff personally delivers the documents to the defendant.

methods.name: Certified mail by clerk
methods.description: Clerk sends the documents via certified mail to the defendant.

service.business_service_rule: Serve the registered agent or an officer of the corporation; for sole proprietors, serve the individual directly.
service.out_of_state_defendant_notes: Service must comply with Nebraska statutes and the laws of the state where the defendant resides.
service.cant_find_defendant_notes: If the defendant cannot be located, service by publication may be an option, but consult the court for guidance.
service.avoiding_service_notes: If the defendant is evading service, document attempts and consider alternative methods permitted by the court.

response_and_default.response_notes: Defendants are not required to file a written answer but should appear at the scheduled hearing.
response_and_default.default_process: If the defendant fails to appear, the court may enter a default judgment in favor of the plaintiff.
response_and_default.prove_up_notes: Even if the defendant doesn't appear, the plaintiff must present evidence to support their claim.

motion_to_vacate_form.name: Motion to Set Aside Default Judgment

response_and_default.motion_to_vacate_appeal_notes: If the motion to vacate is denied, the defendant may appeal the decision within 30 days.

counterclaim.transfer_to_higher_court_notes: If the counterclaim exceeds the small claims limit or the defendant wishes to have legal representation, the case may be transferred to county court.

hearing.lawyers_allowed_notes: Attorneys are not permitted to represent parties in small claims court.
hearing.format: Bench trial with informal procedures; each party presents their case directly to the judge.
hearing.interpreter_request_process: Request an interpreter through the court clerk's office as soon as possible before the hearing.
hearing.decision_timing: On the spot or mailed within a few days.
hearing.on_site_mediation_notes: Some counties offer mediation services on the day of the hearing; check with the local court.

appeals.type_notes: The appeal is a new trial in the district court.
appeals.default_judgment_notes: A default judgment can be appealed within 30 days.

collection.post_judgment_interest_notes: Interest accrues on unpaid judgments at a rate of 12% per annum.

methods.name: Wage garnishment
methods.blurb: Have a portion of debtor's wages withheld until the judgment is paid.
methods.description: File a writ of garnishment with the court, serve the employer, and the employer withholds a portion of the debtor's wages to satisfy the judgment.
methods.effectiveness_notes: Most effective when debtor is W-2 employed at a stable job.

methods.name: Bank levy
methods.blurb: Seize funds from debtor's bank account to satisfy the judgment.
methods.description: Obtain a writ of execution from the court, serve the bank, and the bank freezes and transfers funds to satisfy the judgment.
methods.effectiveness_notes: Effective if debtor has sufficient funds in the account.

methods.name: Property lien
methods.blurb: Place a lien on debtor's real property to secure the judgment.
methods.description: File a certificate of judgment with the county recorder to place a lien on the debtor's property, which must be satisfied before the property can be sold or refinanced.
methods.effectiveness_notes: Effective if debtor owns real property; may take time to realize payment.

methods.name: Writ of execution
methods.blurb: Seize and sell debtor's personal property to satisfy the judgment.
methods.description: Obtain a writ of execution from the court, the sheriff seizes debtor's non-exempt personal property, sells it at auction, and applies proceeds to the judgment.
methods.effectiveness_notes: Effective if debtor owns valuable non-exempt personal property.

collection.judgment_renewal_process: File a motion to revive the judgment before it expires; if granted, the judgment is extended for another 5 years.
collection.bankruptcy_effect_notes: Filing for bankruptcy imposes an automatic stay on collection efforts; certain judgments may be discharged in bankruptcy.
collection.priority_notes: Priority among multiple judgment creditors is generally determined by the order in which liens are filed.

businesses.self_representation_notes: Businesses can be represented by an officer or employee without an attorney in small claims court.
businesses.fictitious_name_notes: Businesses operating under a trade name must file a Certificate of Trade Name with the Secretary of State.
businesses.sole_proprietor_employee_exception: Sole proprietors may represent themselves or authorize an employee to appear on their behalf.
businesses.licensing_notes: Unlicensed contractors may face challenges in enforcing contracts in court.

pitfalls.title: Exceeding the jurisdictional limit
pitfalls.what_happens: Filing a claim over $7,500 in small claims court will result in dismissal or transfer to a higher court.
pitfalls.how_to_avoid: Ensure your claim does not exceed the $7,500 limit; if it does, file in county or district court.

pitfalls.title: Suing the wrong party
pitfalls.what_happens: Naming the incorrect defendant can lead to dismissal and wasted time.
pitfalls.how_to_avoid: Verify the legal name and address of the party you're suing before filing.

pitfalls.title: Missing the statute of limitations
pitfalls.what_happens: Filing after the deadline results in automatic dismissal of your case.
pitfalls.how_to_avoid: Check the statute of limitations for your specific claim type before filing.

pitfalls.title: Improper service of process
pitfalls.what_happens: Failure to serve the defendant correctly can delay or dismiss your case.
pitfalls.how_to_avoid: Follow Nebraska's rules for serving process carefully, using authorized methods.

pitfalls.title: Not preparing evidence
pitfalls.what_happens: Lack of evidence can result in losing your case, even if you're in the right.
pitfalls.how_to_avoid: Gather all relevant documents, photos, and witness statements before the hearing.

pitfalls.title: Failing to collect judgment
pitfalls.what_happens: Winning a case doesn't guarantee payment; you may need to take further steps.
pitfalls.how_to_avoid: Learn about post-judgment collection methods like garnishment and liens.

recent_changes.title: Increase in small claims jurisdictional limit
recent_changes.description: The maximum amount for small claims cases increased from $6,000 to $7,500.

faqs.question: Do I need a lawyer?
faqs.answer: No, attorneys are not permitted to represent parties in Nebraska small claims court; you must represent yourself.

faqs.question: How long does it take?
faqs.answer: Small claims cases typically resolve within 30 to 90 days from filing to judgment.

faqs.question: What's the maximum I can sue for?
faqs.answer: As of July 1, 2025, the maximum amount you can sue for in Nebraska small claims court is $7,500.

faqs.question: What happens if the defendant doesn't show up?
faqs.answer: If the defendant fails to appear, the court may enter a default judgment in your favor.

faqs.question: Can I appeal?
faqs.answer: Yes, either party can appeal a small claims judgment to the district court within 10 days.

faqs.question: How long is a judgment good for?
faqs.answer: A judgment in Nebraska is valid for 5 years and can be renewed before it expires.

state_specific_notes: Nebraska prohibits attorney representation in small claims court; parties must represent themselves.
The small claims jurisdictional limit increased to $7,500 effective July 1, 2025.
Defendants can request a transfer to county court if they wish to have legal representation.
Small claims judgments can be appealed to the district court within 10 days.
Post-judgment interest accrues at a rate of 12% per annum on unpaid judgments.

interesting_findings: Nebraska's small claims court offers a streamlined process for resolving disputes up to $7,500 without attorney representation.
The jurisdictional limit increase to $7,500 aligns Nebraska with several other states' small claims limits.
Nebraska's prohibition on attorney representation in small claims court emphasizes accessibility and simplicity for litigants.
